mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.50% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. Lower policy rates compress required returns on equities, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% as of 2016-01-15 indicates deep easing cycle
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with policy rates near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.50% do not exceed baseline returns by at least 50 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion and rising household income expectations. This triggers positive equity risk appetite and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: post-crisis labor market recovery with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances where PAYEMS MoM > 150k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below 500,000 annualized units signals weakening demand expectations and reduced future consumption capacity. This negative demand signal produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 549,000 units as of 2016-01-15, near trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle demand deceleration with housing weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances where HOUST < 500k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month while unemployment rate rises simultaneously, indicating stagflationary pressure. This joint deterioration in supply capacity and labor utilization produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM < -0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 92.54 and UNRATE at 9.5% as of 2016-01-15
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure with supply constraints and labor slack
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances where both conditions trigger, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while housing starts remain above 550,000 units, signaling synchronized demand expansion across consumer and housing sectors. This dual demand strength produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 362,688M and HOUST at 549k as of 2016-01-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances where both conditions trigger, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rises above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, indicating negative real rates. This monetary accommodation in an inflationary environment produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as real asset values appreciate.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.60 and FEDFUNDS at 0.18% as of 2016-01-15
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances where both conditions trigger, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 5.0% while nonfarm payroll employment declines month-over-month, signaling labor market deterioration. This dual labor weakness produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as recession risk premia increase.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.5% as of 2016-01-15 indicates elevated unemployment
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with rising unemployment and job losses
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances where both conditions trigger, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines for three consecutive months below its 12-month moving average, signaling sustained supply-side weakness and reduced productive capacity. This persistent supply deterioration produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average for 3 consecutive months
regime_hint: supply-side contraction with persistent industrial weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances where trigger condition is met, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
